former {{ill||lt=kokuhei chusha|UNKNOWN}} | founded = (trad.) age of the gods | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | annual_festival = Weekend nearest {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886}} | rituals = Tsuridare ritual (day before grand festival)
Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill||lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} or {{ill||lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:周防国|社1]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:周防国|社1]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}{{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209}} Osaki 1690 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|3|27.75|N|131|32|1.15|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729}}
one additional, unidentified deity | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (2 seats, small)
{{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chusha|1=UNKNOWN}} | founded = (trad.) age of the gods | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | annual_festival = Weekend nearest {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886}} | rituals = Tsuridare ritual (day before grand festival)
Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:周防国|社1]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chusha|1=UNKNOWN}} | founded = (trad.) age of the gods | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | annual_festival = Weekend nearest {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886}} | rituals = Tsuridare ritual (day before grand festival)
Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:周防国|社1]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462|1=Chōgen (monk)}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:周防国|社1]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462|1=Chōgen (monk)}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:周防国|社1]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Tamanooya Shrine in Hofu, Yamaguchi|other Tamanooya shrines}} {{Shrine | name = Tamanooya Shrine | image = [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}} Osaki 1690 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|3|27.75|N|131|32|1.15|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}}
one additional, unidentified deity | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (2 seats, small)
{{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chusha|1=UNKNOWN}} | founded = (trad.) age of the gods | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = Weekend nearest {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} | rituals = Tsuridare ritual (day before grand festival)
Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462|1=Chōgen (monk)}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:周防国|社1]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462|1=Chōgen (monk)}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:周防国|社1]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}}[[Category:qq]]

image of entrance (P9721) == * Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg {{commons category|Category:Tamanooya-jinja}} [[EN:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[JA:玉祖神社]] [[ZH:玉祖神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11572731}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Tamanooya Shrine in Hofu, Yamaguchi|other Tamanooya shrines}} {{Shrine | name = Tamanooya Shrine | image = [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}} Osaki 1690 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|3|27.75|N|131|32|1.15|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}}
one additional, unidentified deity | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (2 seats, small)
{{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chusha|1=UNKNOWN}} | founded = (trad.) age of the gods | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = Weekend nearest {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} | rituals = Tsuridare ritual (day before grand festival)
Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462|1=Chōgen (monk)}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:周防国|社1]] [[Category:qq]]

Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Tamanooya Shrine in Hofu, Yamaguchi|other Tamanooya shrines}} {{Shrine | name = Tamanooya Shrine | image = [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}} Osaki 1690 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|3|27.75|N|131|32|1.15|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}}
one additional, unidentified deity | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (2 seats, small)
{{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chusha|1=UNKNOWN}} | founded = (trad.) age of the gods | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = Weekend nearest {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} | rituals = Tsuridare ritual (day before grand festival)
Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462|1=Chōgen (monk)}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:周防国|社1]]

Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462|1=Chōgen (monk)}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:周防国|社1]]

{{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chusha|1=UNKNOWN}} | founded = (trad.) age of the gods | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = Weekend nearest {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} | rituals = Tsuridare ritual (day before grand festival)
Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462|1=Chōgen (monk)}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:周防国|社1]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chusha|1=UNKNOWN}} | founded = (trad.) age of the gods | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = Weekend nearest {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} | rituals = Tsuridare ritual (day before grand festival)
Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462|1=Chōgen (monk)}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Suō Province]]

Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462|1=Chōgen (monk)}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Suō Province]]

Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Tamanooya Shrine in Hofu, Yamaguchi|other Tamanooya shrines}} {{Shrine | name = Tamanooya Shrine | image = [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}} Osaki 1690 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|3|27.75|N|131|32|1.15|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}}
one additional, unidentified deity | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (2 seats, small)
{{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chusha|1=UNKNOWN}} | founded = (trad.) age of the gods | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = Weekend nearest {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} | rituals = Tsuridare ritual (day before grand festival)
Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha and the ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. Its former modern rank was kokuhei chusha; today it is a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of Jinja Honcho. It is regarded as the head shrine of Tamanooya shrines, including other Tamanooya shrines in Hofu and {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} in Osaka. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Q11572729|WD=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} - ancestral deity of the Tamatsukuri clan; deity associated with making magatama at the time of the Heavenly Rock Cave myth. * One additional deity (unidentified; some theories suggest {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) === History === The shrine is thought to have been founded by jewel-crafting groups (tamatsukuri-be) who worshiped Tamanooya-no-Mikoto. According to one tradition, Emperor Keiko prayed for victory here during his western campaign. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb. In the Heian period it was revered as the ichinomiya of Suo. In modern times it was ranked kokuhei shosha in 1871 and promoted to kokuhei chusha in 1915; after WWII it became a Beppyo shrine. The monk {{ill|lt=Chogen|Q679462|WD=Q679462|1=Chōgen (monk)}} is said to have donated shrine buildings and tax-exempt lands in 1195 in thanks for completion of work connected to reconstruction of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367|1=Tōdai-ji}}. The shrine preserves an Important Cultural Property document bearing Chogen's signature. === Divine rank === * 867-03-10: raised from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Third Rank (recorded as "Tamanooya no Kami") * 964: recorded as Junior First Rank === Precinct === File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|First torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama no Iwaya (traditional tomb of Tamanooya-no-Mikoto) === Major rituals === On the evening before the autumn festival, the "Urate" ritual is performed; early on festival day, a rice-offering rite using earthen kettles is held. Both are linked to a tradition that Empress Jingu divined military fortune at this shrine before the Sankan campaign. The sumo-like Urate performance is also called "Urate-zumo" and is designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Yamaguchi Prefecture. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National) === * "Inventory of shrine buildings and treasures for reconstruction of Suo ichinomiya" (dated 1195, signed by Chogen) - designated June 5, 1976 === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National selection) === * Urate sumo of Tamanooya Shrine - selected December 4, 1997 === Yamaguchi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Urate ritual of Tamanooya Shrine (intangible folk cultural property) - designated November 24, 1976 === Hofu City designated cultural properties === * Two swords (craft works) designated March 31, 1969 * Shrine forest designated natural monument (March 3, 1986) === Other === The shrine is also associated with the origin of the locally designated natural-monument breed "Kurokashiwa chicken"; a monument stands in the precinct and several are kept there. === Access === * JR Sanyo Main Line Hofu Station: about 45 minutes on foot * Bocho bus "Sogo Iryo Center-mae" stop: about 20 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related books === * ''Shinto Dictionary'' * ''Jinja Jiten'' * Masaaki Sugata, ''Nihon no Jinja wo Shiru Jiten'' === See also === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine (Yao)|Q11572730|WD=Q11572730|1=Tamanooya Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の重要文化財]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Suō Province]]

Urate ritual (night before grand festival) }} [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Osaki, {{ill|lt=Hofu|Q502209|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. Shrine tradition says Tamanooya-no-Mikoto died in this area and was enshrined here; nearby is "Tama no Iwaya," said to be the deity's tomb.
